> Cursor-side mirror of `/trace`. Source of truth: `.claude/skills/trace/SKILL.md`.


# /trace

Requirements traceability. Single entry point for the `requirements-curator` agent. All reads of the requirements index and all writes (minting, status updates, orphan detection) route through here.

## When to run

- **Before writing a new feature** — mint a requirement if none exists (`/trace --mint "<description>"`)
- **During review** — look up how a REQ links to tests and code (`/trace REQ-TRADING-014`)
- **On `/phase-complete`** — mandatory coverage audit (`/trace --coverage` + `/trace --orphans`)
- **When `pre-commit-requirements-sync` hook surfaces a missing link**

## Modes

### Lookup: `/trace <REQ-ID>`

Returns everything linked to the requirement:

```markdown
# REQ-TRADING-014

**Status:** implemented
**Created:** 2026-05-03 **Implemented:** 2026-05-20
**Owner:** maintainer **Scope:** trading

## Acceptance criteria

<numbered list from the requirement file>

## Tests (N)

- `packages/shared/src/trading-core/risk-sizing.test.ts`
- `apps/daemon/src/__tests__/integration/place-order.test.ts`

## Code (N)

- `packages/shared/src/trading-core/risk-sizing.ts`

## Related

- REQ-TRADING-012 (accepted)
- decisions/0014-risk-based-sizing-formula.md
```

### Orphans: `/trace --orphans`

Dispatches `requirements-curator` for orphan detection:

- **Code orphans** — source files without any inbound `@req:` tag
- **Requirement orphans** — requirements whose linked tests or code no longer exist
- **Test orphans** — tests without `@req:` tags

Output is a per-category list with suggested remediation for each.

### Coverage: `/trace --coverage`

Scope-wise coverage report:

```markdown
# Requirements coverage

| Scope         | Requirements | Implemented | Code files | Test files | Coverage |
| ------------- | ------------ | ----------- | ---------- | ---------- | -------- |
| trading       | 12           | 8           | 14         | 22         | 67%      |
| web-positions | 6            | 4           | 9          | 11         | 67%      |
| ...           | ...          | ...         | ...        | ...        | ...      |
| **Total**     | 54           | 37          | 89         | 128        | 69%      |

## Orphan code (N)

<files without any @req link>

## Orphan requirements (N)

<requirements with broken links>
```

`/phase-complete` requires coverage above a threshold (default 80% within each in-progress scope) before advancing phases.

### Mint: `/trace --mint "<description>"`

Dispatches `requirements-curator` to:

1. Infer the target scope from the description (or prompt if ambiguous)
2. Read `.reqid-counter` for that scope and allocate the next ID
3. Draft a new requirement file from `docs/requirements/_template.md`
4. Return the draft for maintainer review

The draft is in `status: draft` and does not count toward coverage until the maintainer flips it to `accepted`.

### List (default): `/trace`

Dumps `docs/requirements/index.md` and per-scope summaries.

## Procedure (every mode)

1. Verify `docs/requirements/` exists. If not, skill reports "N/A — requirements scaffolding not yet in place (arrives in Sub-phase 10)" and exits
2. Dispatch `requirements-curator` with the appropriate instruction
3. Relay the agent's output

## Bootstrap tolerance

During Phase 1 Sub-phases 1–9, `docs/requirements/` does not exist. Every `/trace` mode no-ops with a pointer to Sub-phase 10. This is per ADR #0002's fail-open posture — tools ship before the infrastructure they operate on.

## Time / cost

Sonnet-tier. Coverage audit on a populated tree: 5–15 seconds. Mint: 2–5 seconds. Lookup: under a second.
